{{Quote|Do you know the name of Belia Darzu? She was a Dark Lord of the Sith who reigned over two centuries ago.|Hetton on Belia Darzu, the leading Sith figure of the Sictis Wars|<ref name="DBROT" />}}
The '''Sictis Wars''' was the name of a two-[[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] subset of the [[New Sith Wars]], a [[Millennium/Legends|thousand-year]] conflict initiated by a [[New Sith|revived empire]] of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith Order]]. Lasting from [[1250 BBY]] to [[1230 BBY]], the beginning of the Sictis Wars marked the rise of a new major Sith figure, the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Belia Darzu]]. In this period, [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Darzu created the defining threat of the Sictis Wars—the [[Cyborg/Legends|organic-metal]] hybrids known as [[technobeast]]s. With an [[Metanecrons|army]] of these [[Mutant|mutated]] [[Sithspawn/Legends|Sithspawn]] creatures bound to the Dark Lord's will, the technobeasts were unleashed upon the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. In the final [[Standard year/Legends|year]] of this period, Darzu was [[Assassination of Belia Darzu|assassinated]] by her former allies, the secretive [[Mecrosa Order]]. The Sith were nonetheless deemed victorious, though without a clear line of succession for a new Dark Lord to assume Darzu's place.

==Prelude==
For three quarters of a [[Millennium/Legends|millennium]] since [[2000 BBY/Legends|2000 BBY]], a conflict known as the [[New Sith Wars]] had been waged throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. The war was initiated by a [[New Sith|resurgent faction]] of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith Order]],<ref name="TEA" /> a sect of [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[Darksider|practitioners]] that opposed the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].<ref name="DSSB-NSW">''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]''</ref> In the later period of the New Sith Wars, the tide of the conflict had been turned against the Republic, strengthening the power of the Sith as a result. Before the Sictis Wars, the last major figure to lead the Sith was the tyrannical [[Darth Rivan]],<ref name="TEA" /> a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] who commanded his own brand of [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]]—the [[Sith battlelord|Battlelords]]. During an [[Battle at Almas|engagement]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Almas]], Rivan unwittingly summoned a [[Wormhole/Legends|wormhole]] that [[Time travel/Legends|transported him through time]] into the future [[Ruusan campaign]], leading to the Sith Lord's demise.<ref name="EOTJ">{{DoD|Echoes of the Jedi}}</ref>

==War==
{{Quote|She was a student of Sith alchemy; it was said she learned the secrets of mechu-deru, the ability to transform the flesh of living beings into metal and machinery. She used this power to create an army of technobeasts: organic-droid hybrids bound to her will.|Hetton, regarding the feats of Belia Darzu during the Sictis Wars|<ref name="DBROT" />}}
[[File:Technobeast-NEGTD.png|thumb|left|200px|The mutated cyborgs known as technobeasts became the signature threat of the Sictis Wars.]]
In [[1250 BBY]] during the last quarter of the New Sith Wars, the beginning of the Sictis Wars saw the rise of a new leader of the Sith,<ref name="TEA" /> a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Shi'ido]] [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] known as [[Belia Darzu]]. Using a hidden [[Belia Darzu's fortress|stronghold]] on the planet [[Tython/Legends|Tython]], [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Darzu experimented with [[Sith alchemy/Legends|Sith alchemy]] and the [[Force power/Legends|Force technique]] known as ''[[mechu-deru]]''. In her efforts to create hybrids of [[Cyborg/Legends|metal and flesh]],<ref name="DBROT" /> she developed the [[nanogene spore]],<ref name="CSWE-NANO">{{CSWECite|II|359|nanogene spore}}</ref> a [[Nanovirus|technovirus]] capable of transforming living organisms into [[Sithspawn/Legends|Sithspawn]] called [[technobeast]]s.<ref name="NEGD" /> Inside Darzu's fortress, beings from several different [[Species/Legends|species]] were subjected to the virus,<ref name="DBROT" /> forming an [[Army/Legends|army]] of technobeasts known as the [[Metanecrons]].<ref name="END" />

Bound to the Dark Lord's will,<ref name="DBROT" /> the technobeasts were unleashed upon the Galactic Republic and the Jedi,<ref name="TEA" /> becoming recognized as the signature horror of the Sictis Wars.<ref name="NEGD" /> In combat, the technobeasts were known to infect their victims with the nanogene spore, assimilating yet more subjects into the [[Legion/Legends|legions]] of [[Mutant|mutated]] cyborgs.<ref name="END" /> One male [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]], however, managed to resist the effects of the technovirus through the power of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="CSWE-TECH" /> Preserving his identity and becoming known as the "[[technobeast Jedi]]," the Knight continued to serve the galaxy for [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] under this new moniker.<ref name="NEGD" />

By the final [[Standard year/Legends|year]] of the Sictis Wars,<ref name="NEGD-ROT">Logically, the alliance between Darzu and the Mecrosa must have fallen apart by 1230 BBY, the confirmed date of Darzu's death according to ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''.</ref> an alliance that was once formed between Lord Darzu and the [[Mecrosa Order]]<ref name="DBROT" />—an underground [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] cult based in the [[Tapani sector/Legends|Tapani sector]]<ref name="END" />—had fallen apart.<ref name="DBROT" /> Despite their former connections with the Sith, the Mecrosa themselves sought to retaliate in response to Darzu's unwanted incursion in their [[Realspace/Legends|territory]].<ref name="END" /> In addition, Lord Darzu was betrayed by her own Sith followers, who would somehow play a role in her impending [[Assassination of Belia Darzu|assassination]].<ref name="VADER">''[[Vader: The Ultimate Guide]]''</ref> To this end, Belia Darzu was [[Poison/Legends|poisoned]] to [[Death/Legends|death]] by the Mecrosa on the planet Tython,<ref name="END" /><ref name="NEGD" /> ending the Sictis Wars in [[1230 BBY]].<ref name="TEA" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|Ultimately, Belia was betrayed and murdered by her own followers. A familiar occurrence in the histories I have read.|Hetton|<ref name="DBROT" />}}
The Sictis Wars, in the face of Belia Darzu's assassination,<ref name="DBROT" /> were ultimately deemed a victory for the Sith. This victory deprived the dark side cult of a clear line of succession, leaving no established individual to seize the mantle of Dark Lord.<ref name="BOS" /> The murder of Lord Darzu sabotaged her technobeast army,<ref name="END" /> halting the production of new Sithspawn hybrids. The end of the Sictis Wars, however, did not permanently bring an end to this signature threat. In the aftermath of the conflict, the Sith successors of Darzu would continue to employ the creatures—albeit in smaller numbers than before—until the New Sith Wars finally ended in [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]].<ref name="NEGD" /><ref name="TEA" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Sictis Wars were first mentioned as an unidentified period of the New Sith Wars in ''[[Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties]]'', a [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] article written by [[Abel G. Peña]] in [[2006]].<ref name="END" /> This period was identified as the Sictis Wars by author [[Daniel Wallace]] in ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'', a reference book published by [[Del Rey]].<ref name="NEGD" />
